title: cymopterus glomeratus var. parryi
intro: Cymopterus glomeratus var. parryi, also known as Parry's woolly marigold, is a variety of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Cymopterus glomeratus var. parryi include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and stems can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and proper uses.
